takeaway: One company can sell ==five different AIs==. Here's the map.
intro: "Wait — Claude and Claude Code are different things?" Yes, and every company does this. Two ideas untangle all of it: each company has models (the engines) and products (the vehicles built around them).

companies.modelsNote: Their engines are named Claude Fable, Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku — you pick between them inside the products.

products.name: Claude
products.what: The chatbot — on the web, your phone, or desktop. Where everyone starts.

products.name: Claude Code
products.what: An AI programmer that works inside a developer's terminal and editor — it writes and fixes code by itself. Developers' favorite.

products.name: Claude Cowork
products.what: Claude that does hands-on office work — organizing files, drafting documents, working through folders of real work, not just chatting.

products.name: Claude Design
products.what: Makes polished visual things: slides, one-pagers, prototypes. Early experimental release.

products.name: Claude API
products.what: The plug that lets companies build Claude into their own apps.

companies.modelsNote: Their engines are the GPT series (currently the GPT-5 generation, in tiers like Sol, Terra, and Luna).

products.name: ChatGPT
products.what: The chatbot — the most-used AI app in the world.

products.name: Sora
products.what: A separate app for creating AI videos from text descriptions, with a social feed of results.

products.name: ChatGPT Atlas
products.what: A web browser with ChatGPT built into every page — it can read what you're looking at and act on it.

products.name: Codex
products.what: Their AI programmer — writes code on its own inside developer tools.

products.name: OpenAI API
products.what: The plug for companies building ChatGPT-style features into their own apps.

companies.modelsNote: Their engines are the Gemini series (currently Gemini 3.5, in Pro and fast/cheap Flash tiers).

products.name: Gemini
products.what: The chatbot — web, app, and built into Android phones.

products.name: AI Mode in Search
products.what: AI answers inside regular Google Search — the AI many people use without realizing it.

products.name: Gemini in Gmail & Docs
products.what: The same AI woven into Google's email and documents, where your work already lives.

products.name: NotebookLM
products.what: Upload your documents and it becomes an expert on them — summaries, Q&A, even podcast-style audio overviews. A quiet fan favorite.

products.name: AI Studio & Vertex
products.what: The plugs for developers and companies building with Gemini.

companies.modelsNote: Mostly runs other companies' engines (OpenAI's, plus others you can switch between) inside its own products.

products.name: Copilot
products.what: The free assistant built into Windows, Edge, and its own app — for everyday questions.

products.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
products.what: The paid work version inside Word, Excel, Outlook, and Teams — it can use your company's files and email.

products.name: GitHub Copilot
products.what: The original AI programmer, inside code editors — unrelated to the office Copilots despite the name.

companies.modelsNote: Builds its products on a mix of engines from other labs, plus its own.

products.name: Perplexity
products.what: The answer engine — ask a question, get a direct answer with sources attached.

products.name: Comet
products.what: A web browser with the assistant built in — it can browse, summarize, and do tasks on pages for you.
